About: It is about a Spanish board game of the Cross and Circle family.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Cross and Circle family.
ConsistsOf: It consists of four pawns per player.
HasA: It has a cubilete dice cup.
It has four pawns per player.
It has a roll of five.
It has a roll of six.
It has a roll of three 6s.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being license-free in Spain.
It is characterized by the use of dice.
It is characterized by the need for strategy.
It is characterized by the Game of the Goose on the reverse of the board.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Indian game Pachisi.
IsA: It is a Spanish board game.
It is an adaptation of the Indian game Pachisi.
It is a popular game in Spain.
It is still popular especially among adults and seniors.
It is not usually regarded as an abstract strategy game.
It is usually cheaper than a deck of cards.
It is played with one six-sided die.
It is played with four pawns per player.
LocatedIn: It is located in north Morocco specifically Tangiers and Tetouan.
OccurredIn: It occurred in north Morocco specifically Tangiers and Tetouan.
UsedFor: It is used for playing a board game.
It is used for leading pawns out of their nest.
It is used for moving pawns around the whole board.
It is used for moving pawns into the color track.
It is used for moving pawns up to the center of the board.
It is used for chasing opponents.
It is used for eating opponents.
It is used for releasing pawns from the nest.
It is used for advancing pawns.
It is used for blocking opponents.
UsedIn: It is used in game sets.
It is used in real time multiplayer gameplay.
It is used in mobile apps.
UsedTo: It is used to shake and toss the dice.
It is used to release one pawn from the nest.
It is used to move pawns.
It is used to kill an opponent's pawn.
It is used to advance pawns.
It is used to eat opposing pawns.
It is used to form blockades.
It is used to open a blockade.
